label: Uncomplicated scabies without crusted disease or secondary infection
inclusion_criteria: Adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of uncomplicated scabies.
No crusted scabies, institutional outbreak plan, secondary infection, systemic illness, or immunocompromised-host pathway.
exclusion_criteria: Crusted scabies, institutional outbreak, public-health-directed plan, or immunocompromised host.
Secondary bacterial infection, cellulitis, abscess, systemic illness, or severe diffuse rash.
Medication selection or repeat-treatment schedule not supplied by clinician.
default_follow_up: Follow up with primary care, urgent care, or dermatology if symptoms are not improving after treatment or if household spread continues.

source_audit.notes: CDC supports scabies mite infestation framing, intense itching and pimple-like rash symptoms, spread by prolonged skin contact, prescribed scabicide treatment, contact and laundry measures, and special handling for institutional or crusted scabies. Autonomous Phase 1551-1600 approved this narrow v1 only for clinician-diagnosed uncomplicated scabies without crusted disease, outbreak plan, secondary infection, systemic illness, immunocompromised-host pathway, or medication schedule inference.

review.notes: Reviewed as a narrow production ontology phenotype. Autonomous Phase 1551-1600 approved this narrow v1 only for clinician-diagnosed uncomplicated scabies without crusted disease, outbreak plan, secondary infection, systemic illness, immunocompromised-host pathway, or medication schedule inference.
required_modifiers: adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of uncomplicated scabies
no crusted scabies, institutional outbreak, or public-health-directed plan documented
no secondary infection, systemic illness, severe rash mimic, or immunocompromised-host pathway
